name: Cercospora Blight
symptoms: Small circular brown spots with yellow halos on leaves. Infected leaves turn yellow and drop prematurely. Lesions may appear on fruit skin causing surface blemishes.
treatment: Apply copper-based fungicide (Copper Oxychloride 0.3%). Remove and destroy infected plant debris. Improve canopy ventilation by pruning. Spray Mancozeb 0.25% at 15-day intervals during humid seasons.
prevention: Maintain proper plant spacing. Avoid overhead irrigation. Apply preventive fungicide sprays before monsoon season.

name: Fruit Rot (Alternaria)
symptoms: Dark brown to black lesions on fruit surface starting at the calyx end. Internal fruit tissue turns dark and mushy. White or grey fungal growth visible in humid conditions.
treatment: Remove and destroy infected fruits immediately. Apply Carbendazim 0.1% or Thiophanate-methyl 0.05% fungicide. Post-harvest treatment with hot water (52°C for 2 minutes) reduces spread.
prevention: Harvest fruits at proper maturity. Avoid mechanical injury during harvesting. Store in cool, dry conditions.

name: Anthracnose
symptoms: Dark, sunken lesions on fruit surface with orange-pink spore masses in humid conditions. Water-soaked spots that enlarge and turn brown-black. Premature fruit drop and post-harvest fruit rot.
treatment: Apply Carbendazim 0.1% or Mancozeb 0.25% at 10–15 day intervals. Remove and destroy infected fruits and plant debris. Copper-based fungicides (Copper Oxychloride 0.3%) provide effective control. Post-harvest hot water treatment at 52°C for 2 minutes.
prevention: Avoid overhead irrigation and water stagnation. Maintain canopy aeration by regular pruning. Harvest fruits before full ripeness if disease pressure is high. Use disease-free planting material.

name: Bacterial Blight
symptoms: Water-soaked angular leaf spots that turn dark brown. Irregular raised corky spots on fruit rind. Twig die-back from tip downward. Gum exudation from affected branches.
treatment: Prune and destroy infected branches 30 cm below visible infection. Apply Copper Oxychloride 0.3% + Streptomycin Sulphate 500 ppm spray. Bordeaux mixture (1%) gives effective control.
prevention: Use disease-free planting material. Avoid wounding plants during cultural operations. Disinfect pruning tools with 0.5% sodium hypochlorite solution.

name: Phytophthora Root Rot
symptoms: Wilting of shoots and branches. Yellowing of foliage. Dark brown discolouration of roots and crown area. Tree decline and eventual death in severe cases.
treatment: Drench soil with Metalaxyl-M 0.1% or Fosetyl-Al 0.3%. Improve drainage. Remove severely infected plants. Trichoderma-based biocontrol agents applied to soil.
prevention: Ensure well-drained soil. Avoid waterlogging. Plant on raised beds in poorly draining areas.

name: Mealybug Infestation
cause: Insect pest Planococcus citri and Ferrisia virgata
symptoms: White cottony masses in leaf axils, on fruit stalk and calyx. Honeydew secretion leading to sooty mould. Yellowing and wilting of shoots. Fruit blemishes and premature drop.
treatment: Apply Imidacloprid 0.005% or Thiamethoxam 0.01% spray. Release biocontrol agent Cryptolaemus montrouzieri (mealybug destroyer beetle) at 10 beetles per tree. Fish oil rosin soap 3% spray.
prevention: Monitor orchard regularly. Use yellow sticky traps for early detection. Maintain tree hygiene by removing dead bark and weeds.

name: Anar Butterfly (Fruit Borer)
symptoms: Pinhole entry wound on fruit with frass (excrement) outside. Larva bores into fruit, consuming seeds and internal tissue. Damaged fruits show external rotting and drop prematurely.
treatment: Bag individual fruits at marble stage using paper/polythene bags. Spray Chlorpyriphos 0.05% or Spinosad 0.01% at fruit set. Light traps to attract and kill adult moths.
prevention: Harvest fruits at correct maturity — overripe fruits attract more butterflies. Remove and destroy fallen fruits. Use pheromone traps for monitoring adult population.

name: Thrips
symptoms: Silver-grey or bronze discolouration on fruit skin (russeting). Scarring on developing fruits causing cosmetic damage. Leaf curling and distortion of tender shoots.
treatment: Spray Spinosad 45SC 0.01% or Imidacloprid 0.03% at 10-day intervals. Blue sticky traps for monitoring and mass trapping. Neem oil 0.5% spray for organic management.
prevention: Maintain weed-free orchard floor. Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilisation. Monitor with blue sticky traps from flowering stage.
